back_translation: He left from there, went to the region of Judea and the territory on the other side of the Jordan River. The crowd gathered around Him again, and He taught them as He had been accustomed to do.
The Pharisees came to test Him, asking: 'Is it lawful according to the Law for a man to divorce his wife?'
He answered them: 'What did Moses command you?'
They answered: 'Moses permitted writing a certificate of divorce and divorcing her.'
Jesus said to them: 'Because of the hardness of your hearts, Moses wrote this commandment for you.
But from the beginning of creation, 'God made them male and female.'
'For this reason, a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife,
and the two shall become one flesh.' Therefore the two are no longer two, but one flesh.
Therefore, what God has joined together, let no man separate.'
When they returned into the house, the disciples asked Him about this matter again.
He said to them: 'Whoever divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery against her.
And if a woman divorces her husband and marries another man, she commits adultery.'
People brought little children to Him, so that He might touch those children, but the disciples rebuked them.
When Jesus saw this, He was indignant, and said to them: 'Allow the little children to come to me; do not forbid them, for the kingdom of God belongs to those who are like them.
I tell you the truth: whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will not enter into that kingdom at all.'
Then He took those children into His arms, laid His hand on them, and blessed them.
When Jesus had set out on the road, a man ran up and knelt before Him, and asked: 'Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?'
Jesus said to him: 'Why do you call me good? No one is good except God alone.
You know the commandments: Do not murder, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not bear false witness, do not defraud, honor your father and mother.'
The man said to Him: 'Teacher, I have kept all these things from my youth.'
Jesus looked intently at him and loved him, saying: 'You still lack one thing. Go, sell all that you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.'
But that man, when he heard those words, became downcast and went away grieved, because he had many possessions.
Jesus looked around and said to His disciples: 'How difficult it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God!'
The disciples were amazed at His words, but Jesus said to them again: 'Children, how difficult it is to enter the kingdom of God.
It is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.'
The disciples were even more amazed, saying among themselves: 'Then who can be saved?'
Jesus looked intently at them and said: 'For humans it is impossible, but for God it is not so, for all things are possible for God.'
Peter began to say to Him: 'Behold, we have left everything and have followed You.'
Jesus said: 'I tell you the truth: there is no one who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or fields for my sake and for the sake of the gospel
who will not receive a hundred-fold in return in this present time — houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and fields, together with persecutions — and in the age to come, eternal life.
But many who are first will become last, and the last will become first.'

While they were going up to the city of Jerusalem, Jesus went ahead of them. The disciples were amazed, while those who followed behind were afraid. Then He took the twelve disciples aside privately again, and began to tell them what would happen to Him,

saying: 'Behold, we are going up to the city of Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the scribes; they will condemn Him to death, and will hand Him over to the Gentiles.
They will mock Him, spit on Him, scourge Him, and kill Him; and on the third day, He will rise again.'
James and John, the two sons of Zebedee, came to Him and said: 'Teacher, we desire that You do for us whatever we ask.'
He asked them: 'What do you two want me to do for you?'
They said to Him: 'Please grant that one of us may sit at Your right and the other at Your left in Your glory.'
Jesus said to them: 'You two do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink, or be baptized with the baptism that I receive?'
They said to Him: 'We are able.' Jesus said to them: 'The cup that I am about to drink, you also will drink; and the baptism that I receive, you also will receive.
But to sit at my right or at my left is not for me to grant, but is for those for whom it has been prepared.'
When the other ten disciples heard this, they began to be angry with James and John.
Jesus called them to Him and said: 'You know that those who are considered rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.
But it shall not be so among you. Rather, whoever wants to be great among you must be the servant of you all,
and whoever wants to be first among you must be slave of all.
For the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve others, and to give His life as a ransom for many.'
Then they came to the city of Jericho. As Jesus and His disciples and a great crowd were leaving the city of Jericho, Bartimaeus, son of Timaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting beside the road.
When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out loudly: 'Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!'
Many rebuked him to be silent, but he cried out all the more loudly: 'Son of David, have mercy on me!'
Jesus stopped and said: 'Call him.' So they called the blind man, telling him: 'Take courage, get up; He is calling you.'
Bartimaeus then threw off his cloak, jumped up, and came to Jesus.
Jesus asked him: 'What do you want me to do for you?' The blind man said to Him: 'Rabbouni, may I see again.'
Jesus said to him: 'Go your way; your faith has saved you.' Immediately he could see again and followed Him along the road.
